name: AWS NitroTPM (EC2 instances)
description: AWS NitroTPM exposes a virtual TPM 2.0 to EC2 instances on Nitro System hosts. CRTM identifies the AWS hypervisor firmware generation.
secure-boot-default: enabled when NitroTPM is enabled; default Secure Boot db is Amazon-maintained
ek-root-ca-source: AWS publishes the Nitro TPM root CA via aws-nitro-enclaves-attestation endpoints; also available at https://aws.amazon.com/security/ for the Nitro hypervisor attestation root.
nitro-system-note: NitroTPM is orthogonal to Nitro Enclaves — EC2 instance attestation uses NitroTPM; enclave attestation uses a separate AWS-signed document. LapEE verifiers may need to handle both depending on whether the attestation comes from an enclave or the host.
notes: Nitro-specific event log quirk: EBS-backed instances emit an additional EV_SEPARATOR with a custom 8-byte marker instead of the standard 4-byte 0x00000000 (the go-attestation 'ebs_event_missing_eventlog' test vector shows this).
source: AWS EC2 documentation, NitroTPM launch announcements, go-attestation test fixture 'ebs_event_missing_eventlog'.
